Select a mode above, then Start. Auto Fixed/Smart require a live Excel connection.

🚂 Generate Trailer

Define one or more trailer trains. Axle #1 of each train sits at the entered X position; every following axle = previous axle's X − line spacing, all sharing the same Y position and VCG. New trains start with the same line spacing and X position as the trailer above them. Split each train's axle range into axle groups (e.g. axles 1–5 @ 25 t, axles 6–10 @ 50 t) and pick one of the 4 fixed groups for each range from the dropdown — or mark a group No line load for axles that carry no cargo weight. Every range assigned to the same group (even across different trains) shares that one color, shown below, in the preview, and on the 2D/3D dashboard. Rows are written to Load_Editor!B (name, e.g. Group_1_line_1) and D:G starting at row 124 (D = Line load t, E = X, F = Y, G = VCG). Use "Load from Excel" to pull an already-generated trailer back in for editing. Tick "Show preview beside input data" or "Show preview in separate window" below to keep the live graphic visible while you edit.

📋 Load Editor

● Computed here (HyperFormula) — not mirrored from Excel

One workbook, three sheets — TIDE, Load_Editor and CREST_301 — sharing a single embedded HyperFormula engine, exactly like three tabs in a real Excel file. Every cell is editable: type a plain value, or an Excel-style =formula referencing cells on any of the three sheets (e.g. =CREST_301!C4 or =TIDE!F10), calculated live in the browser. On Load_Editor, column D from row 14 (Filling[mT]) and columns D:G from row 124 (RO-RO Line load/Xpos/Ypos/VCG) stay specially linked to the dashboard's live tank/axle data (blue cells) instead of the formula engine. Just like Excel: drag a column header's right edge (or a row number's bottom edge) to resize it, and on Load_Editor, click a cell then drag the small blue square at its bottom-right corner to fill/copy. Every cell edit on every sheet is saved to Firebase automatically, so nothing is lost next time you reopen this page — even outside Excel.

● Standalone mode — editing the embedded copy only (not connected to Excel)
